column2: # We define a complex structure involving almost all variables:
# 1. "Confounders" (Age, Pregnancies, Pedigree) cause BMI and Outcome.
# 2. "Mediators" (Glucose, BP, Insulin) are caused by BMI and cause Outcome.
# 3. We want to know: Is there still a direct arrow from BMI -> Outcome?
# STEP 2: Derive the Adjustment Set (For DIRECT Effect)
# We ask: "What must I control to see the DIRECT effect of BMI,
# blocking the paths through Glucose, BP, etc.?"
print("--- Required Adjustment Set for Direct Effect ---")
"--- Required Adjustment Set for Direct Effect ---"
{ Age, BloodPressure, DiabetesPedigreeFunction, Glucose, Insulin, Pregnancies, SkinThickness }
# We fit the full model with all variables
causal_model <- glm(Outcome ~ BMI + Age + Pregnancies + DiabetesPedigreeFunction +
print("--- Direct Causal Effect of BMI (Coefficients) ---")

glm(formula = Outcome ~ BMI + Age + Pregnancies + DiabetesPedigreeFunction + 
    Glucose + BloodPressure + Insulin + SkinThickness, family = "binomial", 
    data = diabetes_data)
Null deviance: 993.48  on 767  degrees of freedom
Residual deviance: 723.45  on 759  degrees of freedom
# Check if BMI is still significant after giving credit to Glucose/BP/etc.
print("CONCLUSION: BMI has a DIRECT effect on diabetes, distinct from Glucose/BP.")
print("CONCLUSION: BMI acts mostly THROUGH Glucose and other mediators.")
"CONCLUSION: BMI has a DIRECT effect on diabetes, distinct from Glucose/BP."
